activities_projects.title: The Distributive Law Mural
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the distributive property of multiplication over addition
steps: Represent $5 	imes (3 + 2)$ by drawing a $5 	imes 3$ rectangle and a $5 	imes 2$ rectangle side by side
Calculate the area of each and then the total area
Compare it with $5 	imes 5$ and write the generalized rule: $a 	imes (b + c) = (a 	imes b) + (a 	imes c)$
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct grid representation and calculation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Integer Division Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the relationship between multiplication and division of integers
steps: Write three multiplication facts involving negative integers (e.g., $-5 	imes 4 = -20$)
For each fact, derive two corresponding division facts (e.g., $-20 \div 4 = -5$ and $-20 \div -5 = 4$)
Explain how the sign of the quotient is determined
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates multiplication and division rules for integers
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of derived division facts (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Profit and Loss Ledger
activities_projects.objective: Apply integer addition and subtraction to financial data
steps: Create a hypothetical shop ledger for 7 days
Use positive integers for profit and negative integers for loss
Calculate the net profit or loss for the week by summing all integers
Find the average daily profit or loss using division
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies integer operations to basic accounting
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct calculation of net and average values (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Sign Change Challenge
steps: Write expressions like $10 - (-5)$ and $-8 - (-3)$ on cards
Rewrite each expression as an addition problem (e.g., $10 + 5$)
Solve and explain why 'subtracting a negative is like adding a positive'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters the rule of additive inverses in subtraction

activities_projects.title: Integer Magic Square
activities_projects.objective: Practice mental addition of positive and negative integers
steps: Create a $3 	imes 3$ grid
Fill the grid with integers so that every row, column, and diagonal adds up to the same number (e.g., 0 or -3)
Verify the sum of all 8 directions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances speed and accuracy in adding multiple integers
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct construction of a balanced magic square (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Simplification with Brackets
steps: Solve the expression: $-20 + [(-5) 	imes 2] \div (-2)$
Break down the steps showing which operation was performed first
Create your own expression with at least three different operations and solve it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies order of operations to complex integer expressions

activities_projects.title: The Elevation Map
activities_projects.objective: Use integers to measure depth and height
steps: Draw a landscape featuring a mountain (2000m), sea level (0m), and a submarine (-500m)
Calculate the vertical distance between the mountain top and the submarine
If the submarine dives another 200m, show its new position as an integer
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes absolute distance vs. integer position
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct distance calculations using $|a - b|$ (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Multiplication Property Explorer
activities_projects.objective: Verify the Associative property of multiplication
steps: Take three integers $a=-2, b=3, c=-4$
Calculate $(a 	imes b) 	imes c$ and $a 	imes (b 	imes c)$ separately
Repeat with a different set of integers where all three are negative
Write a conclusion on whether the grouping affects the result
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Verifies the associative property for negative numbers

activities_projects.title: The Integer Quiz Master
activities_projects.objective: Test understanding of product and quotient signs
steps: Write 10 problems such as 'Is $(-2) 	imes (-3) 	imes (-4)$ positive or negative?'
Solve them and find a rule for the sign based on the number of negative factors
Exchange with a partner and check answers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Discovers the 'even/odd' rule for multiplying negative integers
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of product signs (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Division by Zero Inquiry
activities_projects.objective: Understand why division by zero is undefined
steps: Try to divide $-10$ by 0 using the reverse multiplication method (i.e., $0 	imes ? = -10$)
Explain why no integer can satisfy this equation
Compare this with $0 \div (-10)$ and explain the difference
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Conceptualizes the meaning of undefined operations
